nodes.title: Data Acquisition and Storage
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Discuss different data sources (databases, APIs, web scraping), data types (structured, unstructured), and basic database concepts including SQL for querying relational databases.

nodes.title: Data Cleaning and Preprocessing
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Focus on handling missing values, outlier detection and treatment, data transformation techniques (scaling, encoding), and basic feature engineering to prepare data for modeling.

nodes.title: Ethics in Data Science
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Discuss critical ethical considerations in data science, including data privacy, algorithmic bias, fairness, transparency, and accountability in AI systems.

nodes.title: Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Teach how to summarize and visualize data to uncover patterns, detect anomalies, and test hypotheses using Python libraries like Matplotlib and Seaborn.

nodes.title: Foundational Mathematics for Data Science
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Cover essential concepts from linear algebra (vectors, matrices, dot products), calculus (derivatives, gradients), and basic probability theory (distributions, Bayes' theorem).

nodes.title: Foundational Statistics for Data Science
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Introduce descriptive statistics (mean, median, mode, variance, standard deviation), inferential statistics (sampling, estimation), and hypothesis testing (p-values, confidence intervals).

nodes.title: Introduction to Data Science
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Define data science, its interdisciplinary nature, common applications, and the data science lifecycle. Discuss different roles within the data science field.

nodes.title: Introduction to Machine Learning
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Define machine learning, differentiate between supervised and unsupervised learning, introduce common ML tasks, and basic concepts of model evaluation, overfitting, and underfitting.

nodes.title: Model Deployment and MLOps Basics
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Provide an overview of how to deploy machine learning models into production environments, basic MLOps concepts, and strategies for model monitoring and maintenance.

nodes.title: Programming for Data Science with Python
nodes.displayTitle: Programming for Data Science with Python
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Cover Python basics, data structures, control flow, and introduce key libraries like NumPy for numerical operations and Pandas for data manipulation.

nodes.title: Supervised Learning: Classification
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Introduce logistic regression, decision trees, random forests, and support vector machines. Focus on classification metrics: accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC curve.

nodes.title: Supervised Learning: Regression
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Cover linear regression, polynomial regression, and introduce evaluation metrics like R-squared, MAE, MSE, RMSE. Implement models using scikit-learn.

nodes.title: Unsupervised Learning: Clustering
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Explore clustering algorithms like K-Means and Hierarchical Clustering. Discuss how to evaluate clustering results using metrics like silhouette score.

nodes.title: Unsupervised Learning: Dimensionality Reduction
nodes.subject: data-science
nodes.description: Introduce Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and other dimensionality reduction techniques. Explain its benefits for visualization and improving model performance by reducing the curse of dimensionality.
